comment.body: haha, you're putting me on the spot :) I read that 10 years ago... I guess my three biggest takeaways were 1. how incredibly sophisticated the business of (American) foreign policy is. The nuances it takes into consideration (ethnic, tribal, economical) 2. how the book predicted in the mid 90s a lot of the major conflicts/themes in the 00s and 2010s and was still so relevant when I read it in 2013 or so. 3. How incredibly complex the dynamics on the whole Eurasian continent are and how knowing/understanding them gives you a better perspective on current events
